facts: >100k stations / 180 countries / ~1.4B daily values / up to 175-yr records; documented thermometer-type changes provide instrument-shift ground truth.
hypotheses: For cross-station shift, the right environment unit is (station × instrument-era), not station alone.
Across decades, instrument and network-density changes dominate the apparent shift more than climate drift.
intuitions: GHCN homogenization literature (Menne, Williams); cross-network comparisons (GHCN-D vs CRUTEM vs HadISD).
unknowns: Instrument-era documentation completeness varies per-station; aggregate metadata can't decide.
Live updates mean dataset stability drifts; whether pinning-to-snapshot is equivalent to retrospective re-analysis cannot be determined.
